2. From [25,26] cols [34,38], UP worked to [20,21] cols [34,38]

This means the corridor at cols [34,38] goes from rows [40,41] up to rows [20,21]. At rows [20,21], the corridor ends (can't go further UP or RIGHT).

- [39,43] → UP → [25,26] cols [39,43]

So the maze has two vertical corridors:
1. Cols [34,38]: rows [20,21] to [40,41]
2. Cols [39,43]: rows [25,26] to [30,31]
These corridors are connected:
- At rows [25,26], cols [34,38] → RIGHT → cols [39,43]
- At rows [30,31], cols [39,43] → DOWN → [29,33] → RIGHT → [34,38]
The white cross is at rows [46,47] cols [51,52], which is in the right room. The right room's opening is at row 40, cols [44,48]. The blocks can't reach this opening from the current corridors.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'O':

        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
